cardTitle: AI and Algorithms Qualification Toolkit (AI AQT)
intro banner: AI AQT is updated according to AI Omnibus amendments to the AI Act and the
intro banner 2: on the implementation of the transparency obligations (Art. 50) published by the European Commission.
description1: AI AQT provides a well-founded assessment whether a data-driven application falls under the AI Act and/or the GDPR, including the associated roles and responsibilities, through dynamic questionnaires.
description6: These dynamic questionnaires are developed for public sector organizations but can also be valuable for other sectors.
feedback: Help develop and share feedback via
save output: Save the outcome for future reference:
error message: An error occurred. No output was found.
disclaimer: Note: Always verify this tool's outcomes with legal expertise and primary sources. This tool reflects Algorithm Audit's interpretation of legal definitions, which remain subject to ongoing debate and evolving guidance. No rights can be derived from these outcomes.
summary intro: Based on your answers, the following regulatory frameworks are applicable:
summary none: Based on your answers, no policy frameworks are applicable.
summary next steps: The table below outlines the next steps you should take:
next step ai: Complete the Risk category questionnaire to determine which AI Act requirements apply.
next step algo: Register the algorithm in the Algorithm Register and verify internal algorithm policy.
next step gdpr: Comply with the data protection requirements of the GDPR.
next step sadm: Consult legal experts to verify whether sADM under Art. 22 GDPR applies.
next step profiling: Comply with profiling requirements under Art. 4(4) GDPR.
article gdpr art22 label: Art. 22 GDPR - Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
intro text: By answering approximately 10 questions in this questionnaire, it is determined which laws and regulations your algorithmic application falls under.
intro next steps note: Based on the results of this questionnaire, next steps are provided to support legal compliance.
intro concepts label: The following concepts and policy frameworks are identified:
intro user guidance gpai: General Purpose AI (GPAI) models, and corresponding risk measures, are out of scope of this tool.
intro concept genai: Transparency requirements for certain AI systems (Art. 50)
intro ai note: Based on analysis of uploaded documents, questions may have been pre-filled by AI. Verify whether the suggested answers are correct.
meta data ai note tooltip: AI will assess whether questions from the compliance questionnaires can be answered and pre-filled with confidence, based on the user's input and uploaded documents. If the AI is uncertain about an answer, that question will be left blank. All pre-filled answers are clearly flagged for the user for verification.
questionnaire 4 title: Obligations per role, status and risk category
obligations ai system note: The below questions are only relevant for
obligations q1 text: What role do you have in relation to the AI system?
obligations q2 text: What is the usage status of the AI system?
obligations q3 text: What is the risk category of your AI system?
obligations hint prefix: Find out by filling in questionnaire:
obligations select prompt: Select a risk category above to see the resulting obligations.
obligations no role note: Select your role above to see the role-specific obligations.
obligations art50 role note: The Art. 50 transparency obligations apply to providers and deployers. Select Provider or Deployer above to see your specific obligations.
obligations art50_4 deployer only note: These obligations apply to deployers of the AI system. Select Deployer above to see them.
aiact2 conclusion 113 text: Based on your answers, the AI Act does not apply to you, because you are not a provider, deployer, importer or distributor. However, carefully verify whether one of these roles might still apply to you.
aiact2 intro downstream provider prefix: A 'Downstream provider' equals a 'Provider'. Only in case an AI model is used, this distinction is relevant (see
aiact2 intro downstream provider suffix: ). Whether an AI model is used, is determined in questionnaire Risk category.
aiact2 intro guidance bullets: A 'Product manufacturer' equals a 'Provider'. Being a product manufacturer is only relevant if: (i) the high-risk AI system relates to products for which the laws in Annex I Section A apply; (ii) the system is placed on the market or put into service together with the product; and (iii) under the name or trademark of the product manufacturer. In this case the product manufacturer is considered to be the provider of the high-risk AI system (see [Art. 25(3) AI Act](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-25)).
aiact2 intro p1 prefix: This questionnaire only applies if the questionnaire
aiact2 intro p2 prefix: Based on 2-3 questions, it is determined what role you have with respect to the AI system. Possible roles and status are:
aiact2 intro role deployer desc: Natural or legal person using an AI system under its own authority in a professional context (Art. 3(4) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role distributor desc: Natural or legal person in the supply chain that makes an AI system available on the Union market without altering its properties (Art. 3(7) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role importer desc: Natural or legal person established in the Union who places on the market an AI system bearing the name or trademark of a non-EU entity (Art. 3(6) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role private desc: AI system is used in the course of a personal, non-professional activity (Art. 3(4) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role provider desc: Natural or legal person that develops an AI system or general-purpose AI model and places it on the market under its own name or trademark (Art. 3(3) AI Act).
aiact2 intro role representative desc: A natural or legal person located or established in the Union who has received and accepted a written mandate from a provider of an AI system to carry out on its behalf the obligations and procedures established by the AI Act (Art. 3(5) AI Act).
aiact2 intro status in development desc: New AI systems face shorter deadlines depending on the type of AI system (Art. 113 AI Act).
aiact2 intro status in use desc: AI systems already in use before the AI Act entered into force must comply by 2030 at the latest with requirements of the AI Act (Art. 111 AI Act).
aiact2 q12 a1: We only develop the AI system.
aiact2 q12 a10: You are a natural person using an AI system for purely personal non-professional activity.
aiact2 q12 a11: We represent a non-EU person or entity to carry out AI Act obligations.
aiact2 q12 a2: We develop the AI system and others also use it.
aiact2 q12 a3: We develop and use the AI system ourselves.
aiact2 q12 a6: We use an externally developed AI system.
aiact2 q12 a7: We import the AI system from a supplier outside the EU.
aiact2 q12 a8: We buy the AI system from a supplier within the EU and resell it.
aiact2 q12 title: How is the AI system developed or deployed?
aiact2 q12b m1: The name or trademark of your organization is put on the AI system.
aiact2 q12b m2: A substantial modification has been made to the AI system.
aiact2 q12b m2 tooltip: A substantial modification to an AI system is a significant change to its architecture, training data or functionality that materially alters how it performs or the outputs it produces. When a high-risk AI system continues to learn after being placed on the market or put into service, this is not considered a substantial modification, provided the changes were pre-determined at the moment of the initial conformity assessment and described in the initial technical documentation (Article 43(4) AI Act).
aiact2 q12b m3: The intended purpose of the AI system is modified by your organization.
aiact2 q12b title: Does one of the following scenarios apply to your AI system?
aiact2 q12b user guidance: Whether a distributor, importer or deployer becomes a provider under the above scenarios mainly matters for high-risk AI systems. The Risk category questionnaire establishes the system's risk classification. In this questionnaire, it is determined whether a relevant a responsibilities along the AI value chain applies.
aiact2 q13 description: An AI system is considered 'in use' as soon as it is actually applied, including in a pilot or testing. A system is 'in use' if it has effect on work processes.
aiact2 q13 explanation intro: This matters because different compliance deadlines apply for AI Act requirements:
aiact2 q13 explanation item0: The AI Act itself doesn't use the phrase "in use". It uses "placed on the market" or "put into service" (as defined in [Art. 3(9)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-3) and [Art. 3(11)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-3)).
aiact2 q13 explanation item1:
AI systems already in use before the AI Act entered into force must comply by 2030 at the latest with requirements of the AI Act.
- A high-risk AI system must have been in use before 2 August 2026 ([Art. 111(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-111)).
- High-risk AI systems in use by public authorities must comply with the AI Act's requirements by 2 August 2030 ([Art. 111(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-111)).
aiact2 q13 explanation item2: New AI systems face shorter deadlines depending on the type of AI system.
aiact2 q13 title: Is the AI system already in use?
aiact2 result ai system status prefix: The status of the AI system is:
aiact2 result in development text: This means shorter compliance deadlines (before 2030) apply, depending on the type of application.
aiact2 result in use text: This means you must comply in 2030 at the latest with AI Act requirements.
aiact2 result role definition prefix: The legal definition of this term can be found in:
aiact2 result role definition prefix plu: The legal definitions of these terms can be found in:
aiact2 result role identified prefix: Based on your answers, you have been identified as a
aiact2 result role identified suffix: in relation to the AI system.
article art113 label: Art. 113 AI Act - Entry into force and application
article art25 label: Art. 25 AI Act - Responsibilities along the AI value chain
ai1 intro text: By answering between 5-15 questions, the risk category of your AI system is determined according to the AI Act.
ai1 intro next steps note: Based on the results of this questionnaire, next steps are provided to support legal compliance.
ai1 intro outcome low: No requirements from the AI Act apply to this AI system (Art. 5, 6 AI Act).
ai1 intro outcome forbidden: AI system meets definition of a prohibited AI system (Art. 5 AI Act).
ai1 intro outcome high: AI system is a high-risk AI system (Art. 6 + Annex III AI Act).
ai1 intro outcome exception: For some domains in which AI systems are used an exception applies, such as scientific research, defense, national security (Art. 2 AI Act).
article art16 label: Art. 16 AI Act - Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
article art23 importer label: Art. 23 AI Act - Obligations of importers
article art26 deployer label: Art. 26 AI Act - Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
article art9 label: Art. 9 AI Act - Risk management system
article art24 label: Art. 24 AI Act - Obligations of distributors
questionnaire AI6 title: Registration of high-risk AI system in EU database (coming soon)
questionnaire AI7 title: Training of staff and others (coming soon)
aiact2 result private no obligations: There are no obligations for private individuals using an AI system for personal, non-professional activities under the EU AI Act.
aiact2 summary title: Overview of roles and responsibilities for high-risk AI systems
aiact2 summary col qms: Quality management system, including post-market monitoring
aiact2 summary col instructions: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
aiact2 summary col oversight: Human oversight and meaningful human intervention
aiact2 summary cell provider postmarket: ✅ Establishes monitoring system; reports serious incidents (Art. 72–73)
aiact2 summary cell deployer postmarket: Monitors use per instructions; reports incidents to provider & authorities (Art. 26(5))
aiact2 summary cell importer postmarket: Reports non-compliance & serious incidents to provider & authorities (Art. 23(6–7))
aiact2 summary cell distributor conformi: Verifies CE marking & Declaration of Conformity (Art. 24(1))
aiact2 summary cell distributor postmark: Reports non-compliance & serious incidents to provider or importer (Art. 24(4–5))
aiact2 result note bullet1 before: The above responsibilities are a selection. More information can be found in:
aiact2 summary guidance:
Product manufacturers are treated as providers. According to Art. 2(1)e, a 'product manufacturer' is an operator that places an AI system on the market or puts it into service alongside its product, under their own name or trademark.

Authorized representatives are treated as importers. An 'authorised representative' means a natural or legal person located or established in the EU who has received and accepted a written mandate from a provider of an AI system or a general-purpose AI model to, respectively, perform and carry out on its behalf the obligations and procedures established by the AI Act.
aiact2 result roles label: Your role(s) under the AI Act:
aiact2 result distributor step1: Verify before distributing that the high-risk AI system bears the required CE marking, is accompanied by the EU Declaration of Conformity and instructions for use, and that provider/importer obligations have been met
aiact2 result distributor step2: Do not distribute a non-compliant system
aiact2 result distributor step3: Ensure proper storage and transport conditions do not affect conformity
aiact2 result distributor step4: Report non-compliance to the provider or importer, and where a risk is present, to competent authorities
aiact2 result distributor step5: Take corrective action (recall, withdrawal) where necessary post-distribution
aiact2 result distributor step6: Cooperate with national authorities upon request
aiact2 result distributor source prefix: The legal text in the AI Act about these obligations can be found in:
aiact2 result provider step_art9: Risk management system: Establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system throughout the entire lifecycle of the high-risk AI system (Art. 9)
aiact2 result provider step_art10: Data and data governance: Ensure training, validation and testing data sets are relevant, sufficiently representative and, to the best extent possible, free of errors (Art. 10)
aiact2 result provider step_art15: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity: Design and develop the system to achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity throughout its lifecycle (Art. 15)
aiact2 result provider step1: Technical documentation: Prepare and maintain full technical documentation (Annex IV) before placing on the market (Art. 11)
aiact2 result provider step2: Conformity assessment: Carry out the applicable conformity assessment procedure (self-assessment or third-party notified body, depending on system type) (Art. 43)
aiact2 result provider step3: CE marking: Affix CE marking to indicate conformity with the Act (Art. 48)
aiact2 result provider step4: EU Declaration of Conformity: Draw up and sign the declaration (Art. 47)
aiact2 result provider step5: Registration: Register the system in the EU database before placing on the market (Art. 49)
aiact2 result provider step6: Quality management system, including post-market monitoring: Establish and maintain a QMS (Art. 17)
aiact2 result provider step7: Record-keeping and logging: Retain automatically generated logs for at least 6 months (or as required by law) (Art. 12)
aiact2 result provider step8: Post-market monitoring: Establish a monitoring system; report serious incidents without undue delay (Art. 72)
aiact2 result provider step9: Transparency and provision of information to deployers: Provide clear instructions to deployers (Art. 13)
aiact2 result provider step10: Human oversight and meaningful human intervention: Build in technical measures enabling effective human oversight (Art. 14)
aiact2 result provider step11: AI literacy: Ensure staff have adequate AI literacy (Art. 4)
aiact2 result provider source prefix: The legal text in the AI Act about these obligations can be found in:
aiact2 result provider heading: Obligations for provider of a high-risk AI system:
aiact2 result provider heading section a: Obligations for provider of a high-risk AI system (in addition to those imposed by the sectoral legislation):
aiact2 result importer heading: Obligations for importer of a high-risk AI system:
aiact2 result distributor heading: Obligations for distributor of a high-risk AI system:
aiact2 result deployer heading: Obligations for deployer of a high-risk AI system:
aiact2 result deployer heading section a: Obligations for deployer of a high-risk AI system (in addition to those imposed by the sectoral legislation):
aiact2 result deployer step1: Transparency and provision of information to deployers: Use the AI system in accordance with the instructions for use; take appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure compliance (Art. 26(1))
aiact2 result deployer step2: Human oversight and meaningful human intervention: Assign human oversight to persons with the necessary competence, training and authority (Art. 26(2))
aiact2 result deployer step3: Data and data governance: Where the deployer controls input data, ensure it is relevant and sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose (Art. 26(4))
aiact2 result deployer step4: Risk management system: Monitor operation of the system on the basis of instructions for use; report identified risks or serious incidents to the provider, distributor and market surveillance authority without undue delay (Art. 26(5))
aiact2 result deployer step5: Record-keeping and logging: Keep automatically generated logs for at least 6 months (Art. 26(6))
aiact2 result deployer step6: Workers: Inform workers' representatives and affected workers before putting the system into service at the workplace (Art. 26(7))
aiact2 result deployer step7: Transparency to affected persons: Inform natural persons that they are subject to a high-risk AI system (Art. 26(11))
aiact2 result deployer step8: Fundamental rights impact assessment: Carry out a fundamental rights impact assessment before deployment (applies to public bodies and certain private entities deploying systems listed in Annex III) (Art. 27)
aiact2 result deployer source prefix: The legal text in the AI Act about these obligations can be found in:
aiact2 result importer step1: Verify before placing on the market that the provider has carried out the conformity assessment, drawn up the technical documentation, and affixed the CE marking
aiact2 result importer step2: Verify that the provider has appointed an Authorised Representative (where required)
aiact2 result importer step3: Ensure the AI system bears the required CE marking and is accompanied by the EU Declaration of Conformity and instructions for use
aiact2 result importer step4: Do not place a non-compliant system on the market
aiact2 result importer step5: Maintain a copy of the EU Declaration of Conformity for 10 years
aiact2 result importer step6: Report any non-compliance or serious risk to the provider and competent authorities
aiact2 result importer step7: Cooperate with national authorities and provide information upon request
aiact2 result importer source prefix: The legal text in the AI Act about these obligations can be found in:
riskcat result more info prefix: More information can be found in:
riskcat result because: Because the following conditions are met:
riskcat result prohibited guidelines suf: as published by the European Commission.
riskcat result prohibited next steps pro: This AI system must be taken out of service immediately. Its intended use constitutes a prohibited AI practice under the EU AI Act and may not be placed on the market, put into service, or used within the European Union.
riskcat result prohibited next steps dep: Stop using the AI system. Inform the provider of the system that must be taken out of service immediately. Its intended use constitutes a prohibited AI practice under the EU AI Act and may not be placed on the market, put into service, or used within the European Union.
riskcat result prohibited obligation: Don't put the AI system on the market, in service, or in use within the European Union.
riskcat result annex i secB intro: Because your AI system falls under Annex I Section B sectoral legislation and requires a third-party conformity assessment, only the following provisions of the AI Act apply directly:
riskcat result annex i secB art6_1: Article 6(1) AI Act: classifies the AI system as a high-risk AI system because it is, or is a safety component of, a product covered by the Annex I Section B legislation that requires a third-party conformity assessment.
riskcat result annex i secB art102_109: Articles 102 to 109 AI Act: amend the relevant Annex I Section B regulations (Civil Aviation Security, two- or three-wheel vehicles, agricultural and forestry vehicles, marine equipment, rail interoperability, motor vehicles approval, motor vehicles general safety type-approval, and Civil Aviation/EASA) to integrate AI-specific requirements into the existing sectoral framework.
riskcat result annex i secB art112: Article 112 AI Act: subjects the AI Act to periodic Commission evaluation and review.
riskcat result annex i secB sectoral: The detailed AI Act obligations of Articles 8 to 17 (data governance, technical documentation, record-keeping, transparency, human oversight, accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity, quality management, etc.) do not apply directly under the AI Act – they are integrated into the sectoral regulation governing your product. See [Art. 2(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-2).
riskcat result annex i secB deployer onl: The AI Act does not impose any new obligations on deployers beyond those already set out in the sectoral framework. See [Art. 2(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-2).
riskcat result annex i secB provider onl: The AI Act does not impose any new obligations on providers beyond those already set out in the sectoral framework. See [Art. 2(2)](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-2).
ai1 intro role missing prefix: Obligations depend on the role you have in relation to the AI system. Fill in questionnaire
article art50_1 label: Art. 50(1) AI Act - Transparency for interactive AI systems
article art50_2 label: Art. 50(2) AI Act - Marking of AI-generated content
article art50_3 label: Art. 50(3) AI Act - Disclosure for emotion recognition and biometric categorisation
article art50_5 label: Art. 50(5) AI Act - Timing and form of the disclosure
article art50_4 label: Art. 50(4) AI Act - Disclosure for deep fakes and AI-generated public-interest text
riskcat result art50_1 core obligation h: Obligations for AI interacting directly with natural persons:
riskcat result art50_1 core obligation s: Design and develop the AI system so that natural persons are clearly informed they are interacting with an AI system. Unless this is obvious from the perspective of a reasonably well-informed natural person, taking into account the circumstances and context of use.
The notification must be embedded in design and provided at the latest at the time of the first interaction (Art. 50(5)).
Information must be clear, distinguishable, accessible (incl. for vulnerable groups: children, elderly, persons with disabilities) and given in a context-appropriate manner.
riskcat result art50_1 deployer step1: Verify that the provider has implemented the disclosure of interactive AI (e.g., an opening line from a chatbot, a clear "AI assistant" label, or equivalent signal).
riskcat result art50_1 deployer step2: Double check that your own configuration, branding, or integration of the system does not undermine or remove the provider's disclosure. If you fine-tune, rebrand, or substantially modify the system, Article 25 may convert you into a provider, importing full Article 50(1) provider duties.
riskcat result art50_1 guidelines title: Guidelines on the implementation of the transparency obligations
riskcat result art50_1 guidelines accept: Textual labels/banners (e.g. 'You are interacting with an AI system'), first-turn chatbot greetings, persistent UI badges.
Auditory cues in voice/telephony contexts, with periodic reminders in long interactions.
Visual/graphical cues (icons, watermarks, recognisable AI symbols).
riskcat result art50_1 guidelines insuff: Disclosure only in T&Cs, URLs or documentation.
Machine-readable markings only (not perceivable at the point of interaction).
Vague terms like 'assistant' or human-like representations that may mislead.
Purely technical descriptions (e.g. 'this system uses LLMs') without explaining the AI nature.
riskcat result art50_1 guidelines source: Draft Guidelines on the implementation of the transparency obligations for certain AI systems under Article 50 of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act)
riskcat result art50_2 core obligation h: Obligations for AI generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content:
riskcat result art50_4 core obligation h: Obligations for AI generating or manipulating image, audio, video or public-interest text content:
riskcat result art50_2 core obligation s: Marking: outputs must be marked in a machine-readable format (e.g. watermarks, metadata, cryptographic provenance methods, fingerprints).
Detection: providers must make means available to detect that the output is AI-generated or manipulated, with human-readable results.
Both elements must be present; marking alone is insufficient.
riskcat result art50_2 deployer step1: Deployers of AI systems that generate deep fakes (as defined in Art. 3(60)) must disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated.
riskcat result art50_2 deployer step2: Deployers of AI systems that generate or manipulate text which is published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest must disclose that the text has been artificially generated or manipulated.
riskcat result art50_2 deployer exceptio: Where the use is authorised by law to detect, prevent, investigate, or prosecute criminal offences.

Where the content forms part of an evidently artistic, creative, satirical, fictional, or analogous work or programme. In this case, the obligation is limited to disclosing the existence of such generated or manipulated content in an appropriate manner that does not hamper the display or enjoyment of the work.
Where the AI-generated content has undergone a process of human review or editorial control, and a natural or legal person holds editorial responsibility for the publication.

This is the relevant provision for journalism, news reporting, public communications, political messaging, and similar contexts. It does not cover routine business writing, marketing copy, internal documents, or fiction.
riskcat result art50_2 guidelines title: Guidelines on the implementation of the marking and detection obligations
riskcat result art50_2 guidelines qualit: Effective — enables natural persons to distinguish AI-generated/manipulated content.
Reliable — accurately identifies AI content under nominal conditions.
Robust — maintains performance under varying conditions and adversarial attacks.
Interoperable — works seamlessly across systems, actors and contexts.
Solutions must be technically feasible and aligned with the state of the art; combining techniques is generally required.
riskcat result art50_2 guidelines scope: Covers text, images, audio, video (incl. multimodal, 3-D, VR/AR, digital twins).
Covers both generation and manipulation that goes beyond standard editing.
riskcat result art50_2 guidelines outofs: Outputs that merely arrange/select existing content (e.g. recommender systems, music playlists).
Mere observations/recordings (e.g. sensor data), short numeric/symbol outputs, source code, machine-to-machine outputs, closed-loop industrial outputs.
riskcat result art50_2 guidelines except: Assistive function for standard editing (grammar/spell-check, format conversion, minor cropping, noise reduction, AAC tools for disabilities).
AI systems that do not substantially alter input data or its semantics.
Systems authorised by law for detection, prevention, investigation or prosecution of criminal offences.
Narrow cases for closed industrial/B2B applications and ephemeral real-time content (e.g. video games) where users are aware.
riskcat result art50_2 guidelines source: Draft Guidelines on the implementation of the transparency obligations for certain AI systems under Article 50 of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act)
riskcat result art50_3 core obligation h: Obligations for AI recognising emotions or categorising persons based on biometric data:
riskcat result art50_3 deployer core ite: Inform all natural persons exposed to the system that it is being operated/used.
Provide information in a clear, distinguishable and accessible manner (Art. 50(5)).
Information may be given in writing, via standardised icons, orally, electronically or any combination, depending on the deployment context.
riskcat result art50_3 deployer timing i: Provide information to users at the latest at the time of the first exposure to the system.
riskcat result art50_3 deployer exceptio: Systems permitted by law to detect, prevent or investigate criminal offences, with appropriate safeguards under Union law.
riskcat result art50_3 provider obligati: Inform exposed persons and process personal data per GDPR.
riskcat result art50_2 clause: generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content
riskcat result art50_3 clause: recognising emotions or categorising persons based on biometric data
riskcat result art50_4 clause: generating or manipulating image, audio, video or public-interest text content
